originalTitle: GOG apologizes for emailing people Nazi symbols
summary: GOG sent a newsletter about the game The End of the Sun on June 5th that included symbols associated with the Nazi SS. The Steam competitor issued a statement attributing the inclusion to a "series of mistakes," including miscommunication with the German QA team, inconsistent font rendering, and being understaffed during a bank holiday, among […]
content: GOG sent a newsletter about the game The End of the Sun on June 5th that included symbols associated with the Nazi SS. The Steam competitor issued a statement attributing the inclusion to a “series of mistakes,” including miscommunication with the German QA team, inconsistent font rendering, and being understaffed during a bank holiday, among other things. The game is a fantasy title set in a universe based on Slavic mythology and culture. So GOG included Slavic runes in its email promoting the title, including the Sowilō symbol, which means “sun.” According to one of GOG’s multiple explanations, some platforms, including mobile phones, rendered the rune as ϟ. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/HKE9aJqW8AAL7Qa.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] Adding confusion to the situation is a statement posted on Reddit by a verified GOG rep saying: > “I also recognize that placing two such runes next to each other could create an unfortunate association with symbols used by the Nazi regime. This was noticed before distribution, and out of respect for local sensitivities, the material was not sent to the German community.” This has led many users to question why the newsletter was sent out to other localities or why the symbol was even doubled up in the first place.  [Media: https://twitter.com/GOGcom/status/2062981104362242551] The developers of the game have also expressed confusion, posting in a Reddit thread, “We don’t know why GOG used symbols in their newsletter. Really, we were also totally surprised when we saw the logo in the newsletter (the old logo) and the symbols next to the newsletter title.”
chineseTitle: GOG 为向人们发送纳粹符号的电子邮件而道歉
chineseSummary: GOG 于 6 月 5 日发送了一份有关《太阳的尽头》游戏的时事通讯，其中包含与纳粹党卫军相关的符号。 Steam 竞争对手发表声明，将其归咎于“一系列错误”，包括与德国 QA 团队沟通不畅、字体渲染不一致以及银行假期期间人手不足等。
chineseContent: GOG 于 6 月 5 日发送了一份有关《太阳的尽头》游戏的时事通讯，其中包含与纳粹党卫军相关的符号。 Steam 竞争对手发表声明，将其归咎于“一系列错误”，包括与德国 QA 团队沟通不畅、字体渲染不一致以及银行假期期间人手不足等。该游戏是一款以斯拉夫神话和文化为背景的奇幻游戏。因此，GOG 在宣传该游戏的电子邮件中加入了斯拉夫符文，其中包括 Sowilō 符号，意思是“太阳”。根据GOG的多种解释之一，包括手机在内的一些平台将符文渲染为ϟ。 [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/HKE9aJqW8AAL7Qa.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] 一位经过验证的 GOG 代表在 Reddit 上发表的声明让情况更加混乱，他说：>“我还认识到，将两个这样的符文放在一起可能会与纳粹政权使用的符号产生不幸的关联。这是在分发之前注意到，出于对当地敏感性的尊重，该材料没有发送给德国社区。”这导致许多用户质疑为什么时事通讯被发送到其他地方，或者为什么这个符号一开始就被加倍了。  [媒体：https://twitter.com/GOGcom/status/2062981104362242551] 游戏开发者也表达了困惑，他们在 Reddit 帖子中发帖称，“我们不知道为什么 GOG 在他们的时事通讯中使用符号。说实话，当我们看到时事通讯中的徽标（旧徽标）以及时事通讯标题旁边的符号时，我们也感到非常惊讶。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
